prs.title: Fix os.userInfo() returning "unknown" for username and shell
prs.body:
_Closes_ #25171

📝 Description

This PR fixes incorrect behavior in os.userInfo() where username and shell were returned as "unknown" in container environments. It implements proper passwd database lookup using uv_os_get_passwd() and memory cleanup with uv_os_free_passwd(). userInfo() now reads username, shell, UID, GID, and homedir from /etc/passwd, with fallbacks to USER and SHELL environment variables to ensure compatibility across different Unix systems.

📓 References

  • Fixes issues where child_process.spawn() failed due to missing shell

  • Related to behavior differences vs Node.js passwd lookup

  • No new related issues documented yet (can add link if exists)

📦 Dependencies & Requirements

  • No new dependencies introduced

  • Works on POSIX systems using getpwuid_r()

  • Ensures graceful fallback when passwd fields are missing (e.g., minimal containers)

📊 Contributor Summary

Contributor | Lines Added | Lines Removed | Files Changed -- | -- | -- | -- @nihalchollampat | Auto-filled by CodeRabbit | Auto-filled | Auto-filled

✔️ Additional Notes

  • Major improvement for container reliability (Fedora, Alpine, etc.)

  • Behavior now aligned with Node.js expectations

  • No regression risk found when tested on standard Linux desktop systems

prs.title: Clarify that libXScrnSaver is not required for Electron apps on Linux
prs.body:
Description of Change
Follow-up to #48989 
This PR updates the Linux build instructions to clarify that libXScrnSaver (libxss) is not required for Electron apps. The powerMonitor module uses DBus for idle detection, making libXScrnSaver unnecessary.

Problem
Electron apps built with electron-builder fail to install on RHEL10 because electron-builder’s defaults include libXScrnSaver as a dependency.
libXScrnSaver is not available on RHEL10 and similar distributions, resulting in installation failures.
Solution
Removed libXScrnSaver from the prerequisites list across all Linux distributions.
Added an explanatory note in the prerequisites section clarifying why it is not needed.
Added a new “Not Required” entry in the runtime dependencies section, with a clear explanation and recommendation to omit it.
Impact
Helps electron-builder and other packaging tools update their defaults, preventing installation issues on RHEL10 and similar environments.
No breaking changes — this is a documentation-only clarification that reflects Electron’s actual runtime behavior.
Checklist
 PR description included and stakeholders cc’d
 npm test passes
 Release notes are relevant, capitalized, punctuated, and past tense
Release Notes
Notes: none

prs.body:
### **Description of Change**

Follow-up to   #48989 
This PR updates the Linux build instructions to clarify that **libXScrnSaver (libxss) is *not* required** for Electron apps. The `powerMonitor` module uses **DBus** for idle detection, making libXScrnSaver unnecessary.

### **Problem**

* Electron apps built with electron-builder fail to install on **RHEL10** because electron-builder’s defaults include libXScrnSaver as a dependency.
* libXScrnSaver is not available on RHEL10 and similar distributions, resulting in installation failures.

### **Solution**

* Removed libXScrnSaver from the prerequisites list across all Linux distributions.
* Added an explanatory note in the prerequisites section clarifying why it is not needed.
* Added a new “Not Required” entry in the runtime dependencies section, with a clear explanation and recommendation to omit it.

### **Impact**

* Helps electron-builder and other packaging tools update their defaults, preventing installation issues on RHEL10 and similar environments.
* No breaking changes — this is a documentation-only clarification that reflects Electron’s actual runtime behavior.

### **Checklist**

* [x] PR description included and stakeholders cc’d
* [x] `npm test` passes
* [x] Release notes are relevant, capitalized, punctuated, and past tense

### **Release Notes**

Notes: none

prs.title: fix(traefik): generate Host rules only when host present and normalize paths #7289
prs.body:
Closes  #7289 
**PR Description**
This PR improves Traefik rule generation by ensuring Host() is only emitted when a host value actually exists, and by normalizing paths so that all middleware, regex, StripPrefix, and router labels use consistent values.
**What’s changed:**
*Added a new buildTraefikRule helper to centralize rule construction.
*Normalized path handling (treat '' and '/' as no-path).
*Ensured PathPrefix() receives a properly normalized path (leading slash enforced).
*Prevented invalid configurations like:   Host("") && PathPrefix(coolify.io). which previously caused 404 routing failures.
*Updated the code to rely on normalized path values from fqdnLabelsForTraefik / fqdnLabelsForCaddy.
**Why**
Previously, empty hosts combined with non-normalized paths resulted in Traefik misrouting — especially cases where the router generated Host("").
This PR eliminates those invalid rules and ensures predictable routing behavior.
**Testing**
Verified rule output from the helper for all combinations:
*host only
*path only
*both
*neither (falls back to PathPrefix(/))
*Confirmed Traefik labels no longer include empty hosts.
*Verified middleware + ghost regex + StripPrefix use normalized paths.
 Files Changed
docker.php

prs.title: Fix Dockerfile ENV format warning and updated configb page #2542
prs.body:
Description:

Closes #2542 
This PR fixes the legacy key/value format warning in docs/Dockerfile by updating the ENV instruction to use the modern key=value format.

Changes:

Updated ENV NODE_ENV production to ENV NODE_ENV=production in docs/Dockerfile#L15
Resolves the warning: "Legacy key/value format with whitespace separator should not be used"
This change ensures compliance with modern Dockerfile best practices as recommended by Docker documentation.

Testing:

Verified the Dockerfile builds successfully after the change
Confirmed the warning is resolved by running Docker linting tools
Related Issues:

Fixes the annotation warning reported in the CI/CD pipeline

prs.title: Clarify Linux Runtime Dependencies
prs.body:
### **Description of Change**

Adds comprehensive documentation of Electron’s **actual Linux runtime dependencies** and clarifies that `libXScrnSaver` / `libXss` is **not required**. This resolves confusion raised in Issue #48989 where downstream packagers (e.g., electron-builder) rely on outdated assumptions, causing installation failures on modern distributions such as RHEL10 where `libXScrnSaver` is no longer available.

A new documentation page (`linux-dependencies.md`) has been added that:

* Lists the core runtime libraries Electron depends on (GTK3, NSS, Nspr, ALSA, PulseAudio, DBus, CUPS, libnotify, X11 extensions, etc.)
* Provides distro-specific package names for major Linux families
* Explains why `libXScrnSaver` is no longer needed and not linked by Electron
* Helps tooling and distro maintainers rely on an authoritative source for dependency requirements

This improves clarity for developers and prevents incorrect dependency declarations in downstream RPM/DEB packaging workflows.

Closes #48989

---

### **Checklist**

* [x] PR description included and stakeholders cc’d
* [x] `npm test` passes
* [x] Documentation added following style guidelines
* [x] No code changes required
* [x] PR release notes added and written for app developers

---
Release Notes
Notes: fix crash when creating event object for ipc events

prs.title: fix: update package-lock.json to reduce security vulnerabilities
prs.body:
Issues Found:
Security Vulnerabilities: 62 vulnerabilities remain in dependencies (4 low, 23 moderate, 29 high, 6 critical). These include critical issues like babel-traverse (arbitrary code execution), elliptic (private key extraction), and form-data (unsafe random). Some were partially fixed by running npm audit fix, reducing from 80 to 62 vulnerabilities, but remaining ones require npm audit fix --force, which may introduce breaking changes.

Modified Files: package-lock.json has changes from the audit fix that need to be committed.

Potential Issues: Deprecated dependencies during npm install.

Issues Resolved:
Tests: Node.js tests passed (237 passing). Browser tests are running and appear to be passing.
Build: Successful build completed.
Version Check: Passed.
ESLint: Ran without visible errors.
Partial Security Fixes: Vulnerabilities reduced from 80 to 62 via npm audit fix.
Committed Changes: package-lock.json updated and committed with message "fix: update package-lock.json to reduce security vulnerabilities from 80 to 62".
Branch Created and Pushed: New branch 'security-fixes' created and pushed to origin.
